summary: Rare early‑Dead set with blues gems and Pigpen’s Big Boy Pete
blurb: The Matrix show delivers a loose, high‑energy blend of blues, folk and early psychedelic R&B. Standout moments include the vocal trade on Big Boy Pete and tight renditions of Me And My Uncle, The Same Thing and Viola Lee Blues.
review: Recorded at San Francisco’s iconic Matrix on November 29 1966, this early Dead outing captures the band still shaping their sound. Pigpen steals the spotlight with a raucous Big Boy Pete, trading verses with Jerry and Bob in a rare vocal showdown that reviewers describe as a high point. The first set rolls through a treasure trove of early material – Me And My Uncle, The Same Thing, Stealin', Early Morning Rain and a gritty Viola Lee Blues – while the third set builds a spirited closing jam with Down So Long, Lindy and I Just Want To Make Love To You. Jerry’s guitar flashes brilliance but occasionally lapses into repetitive licks; Bill’s bebop‑flavored drumming keeps the momentum alive, and Phil holds down a solid bass line even as he steps up for lead vocals when Jerry battles a cold. Overall the performance is vibrant and historically fascinating, making it a must‑listen for fans of the Dead’s formative years despite some recording quirks.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the set’s energy and standout songs; two are mixed but do not contain strong negative criticism of the performance itself. The overall tone is upbeat with consistent highlights across sources, justifying a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with the clearest overall balance; despite minor drop‑outs it captures the full band sound better than other tapes.

key_highlights: Pigpen‑led Big Boy Pete featuring a vocal trade with Jerry and Bob.
Early‑Dead blues/folk blend highlighted by Me And My Uncle, The Same Thing and Viola Lee Blues.
High‑energy closing jam on Down So Long and Lindy.
Bill’s bebop‑style drumming adds extra swing to the set.
song_highlights: I Just Want To Make Love To You

band_performance.Jerry: Shows flashes of brilliance with strong guitar work, though occasional repetitive licks appear; also trades verses on Big Boy Pete and steps back to let Phil sing when ill.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation and steps up for lead vocals during Jerry’s cold, keeping the set grounded.
band_performance.Bob: Delivers energetic rhythm guitar and shines in the vocal trade on Big Boy Pete; some tape‑related recordings note uneven rhythm but overall performance is strong.
band_performance.Keys: Pigpen’s vocals and harmonica drive the standout Big Boy Pete, adding a gritty blues feel to the show.

must_listen_sequences: I Just Want To Make Love To You
